equipment.description: Nuclear reactor where primary coolant is kept under high pressure to prevent boiling.
Nuclear reactor where primary coolant is allowed to boil in the core.
Large heat exchanger used to convert water into steam from heat produced in a nuclear reactor core.
Steam generator where primary coolant flows straight through, transferring heat to secondary side.
Component in a PWR that maintains primary system pressure and accommodates volume changes.
Large vertical pump circulating primary coolant through the reactor core.
High-capacity pump supplying feedwater to the steam generators.
Pump that removes condensed water from the main condenser hotwell.
Pump providing cooling water to various reactor auxiliary components.
Safety-related pump supplying cooling water from the ultimate heat sink.
Pump used to remove decay heat from the reactor core during shutdown.
Pump providing high-pressure coolant injection during emergency conditions.
Pump providing high-volume, low-pressure coolant injection during large break LOCAs.
Pump used to spray water inside the containment to reduce pressure and wash out fission products.
Steam turbine-driven pump providing emergency feedwater to steam generators.
Electric motor-driven pump providing emergency feedwater to steam generators.
Large, fast-closing valve to isolate the steam generator from the secondary plant.
Spring-loaded safety valve to protect the reactor coolant system from overpressure.
Pilot-operated valve used to relieve reactor coolant system pressure.
Fast-closing valve to isolate main feedwater flow to the steam generators.
Valve designed to automatically close and seal the containment building during accidents.
Airtight steel or concrete structure surrounding the reactor to confine radioactive materials.

equipment.type: Spent Fuel Pool Cooling Heat Exchanger
equipment.description: Heat exchanger used to remove decay heat from stored spent nuclear fuel.
Heat exchanger that transfers decay heat from the reactor core to the component cooling water system.
Heat exchanger transferring heat from closed cooling loops to the ultimate heat sink.
Vessel used to remove moisture and reheat steam between high and low-pressure turbines.
Large shell and tube heat exchanger condensing turbine exhaust steam back to water.
Turbine section that receives high-pressure steam directly from the steam generators.
Turbine section utilizing lower pressure steam after it passes through the MSR.
Large synchronous generator driven by the steam turbines to produce electricity.
Diesel-driven generator providing backup power to safety-related equipment.
Alternate AC power source designed to handle complete loss of offsite and emergency power.
Electromechanical device used to position neutron-absorbing control rods in the reactor core.
Vessel containing highly concentrated boric acid for emergency reactivity control.
Surge tank for the chemical and volume control system.
Large borated water storage tank used for emergency core cooling and refueling operations.
Storage tank providing makeup water to the secondary cycle and auxiliary feedwater system.
Pressurized tank containing borated water for rapid passive injection into the reactor core.
Control cabinet housing the logic for automatic reactor trips and safety actuations.
Detector located outside the reactor vessel measuring neutron flux levels.
Miniature detector positioned inside the reactor core for local power mapping.
Temperature sensor monitoring the temperature of coolant leaving the fuel assemblies.
Resistance temperature detector measuring primary coolant temperature.
Instrument monitoring the pressure inside the reactor containment building.
System used to control post-accident hydrogen gas concentrations in containment.
Evaporator used to concentrate liquid radioactive waste.
Compressor used to process and store radioactive gases for decay.
Inverter converting DC battery power to AC for vital instruments and controls.
Medium voltage electrical switchgear distributing power to major safety-related motors.
Safety-grade lead-acid battery bank providing reliable DC power during emergencies.
Large fan unit used to circulate and cool air inside the containment building.
Air conditioning unit providing habitability control for operators during emergencies.
